title: Trajectories of Northern Wheatears breeding in Val Piora
name: val-piora-wheatear

schema.name: measurements
schema.title: Measurements

fields.name: tag_id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the tag. Foreign key to [`tags.tag_id`](/GeoLocator-DP/core/tags#tag_id).

fields.name: sensor
fields.description:
Sensor type. - `light`: Light-level values measured by a solar geolocator. Range and units may vary by provider and tag settings.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000139/ - `pressure`: Atmospheric pressure in hPa (or mbar)
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000055/ - `activity`: Measure of the movement performed by the bird, computed from acceleration sensors. Range and method of calculation may vary by the tag provider, model, settings and placement on the animal. In general, higher values indicate more activity. Typically computed as the cumulative sum of the absolute difference of the acceleration along the z-axis. See [doi:10.1186/s40462-018-0137-1](https://doi.org/10.1186/s40462-018-0137-1) (SOI), [doi:10.1111/jav.01068](https://doi.org/10.1111/jav.01068) (Lund) and [migratetech report](https://www.migratetech.co.uk/IntigeoSummary.pdf) for more details.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000114/ - `pitch`: The pitch of the tag, as measured by the gyroscope, accelerometer and/or magnetometer on the tag. Values are in degree and range from -180 to 180, with positive values indicating backward pitch and negative values indicating forward pitch.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000263/ - `temperature_external`: The temperature measured by the tag's sensor pointing outward from the animal. Also called air temperature. Corresponds typically to most temperature sensor.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000104/ - `temperature_internal`: The temperature measured by the tag's sensor located between the tag and the animal. Also called body temperature.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000256/ - `acceleration_x`: Raw acceleration values provided by the tag for the X axis. Units: m/s^2.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000006/ - `acceleration_y`: Raw acceleration values provided by the tag for the X axis. Units: m/s^2.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000007/ - `acceleration_z`: Raw acceleration values provided by the tag for the X axis. Units: m/s^2.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000008/ - `magnetic_x`: The magnetic field strength measured by the magnetometer for the X axis. Units: microtesla.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000258/ - `magnetic_y`: The magnetic field strength measured by the magnetometer for the Y axis. Units: microtesla.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000259/ - `magnetic_z`: The magnetic field strength measured by the magnetometer for the Z axis. Units: microtesla.
Same as https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000260/

fields.name: datetime
fields.description: Date and time of the measurement. Formatted as an ISO 8601 string with timezone designator `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ`.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Value of the measurement taken by the sensor

fields.name: label
fields.description: Labeling of the pressure and acceleration data according to the [labeling chapter of the GeoPressureManual](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureManual/labelling-tracks.html)

schema.name: tags
schema.title: Tags
schema.description:
`tags.csv` is a [tabular data](https://datapackage.org/standard/glossary/#tabular-data) ressources from a GeoLocator Data Package. As we assumes that a tag is only deployed once on a single species, this table contains informations related to the device, the deployment and the animal equiped. Data collected on the bird which might be changing over time (e.g., `age`) should be provided in the [`observations`](/GeoLocator-DP/GeoLocator-DP/core/observations) table.

Additional data can be provided by following the [Movebank tag attribute dictionary](https://www.movebank.org/cms/movebank-content/movebank-attribute-dictionary#tag_attributes) and [Movebank deployement attribute dictionary](https://www.movebank.org/cms/movebank-content/movebank-attribute-dictionary#deployment_attributes)

fields.name: tag_id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the tag.

fields.name: ring_number
fields.description: Ring number associated with the tagged species. Use `null` if the tag was not deployed.

fields.name: scientific_name
fields.description: Scientific name of the tagged species. Use `null` if the tag was not deployed.

fields.name: manufacturer
fields.description: The company or person that produced the tag. When appropriate, please use 'Swiss Ornithological Institue', 'Migrate Technology Limited', or 'Lund University'.

fields.name: model
fields.description: Model/design/version of the tag. Any specification of the device able to identify it.

fields.name: firwmare
fields.description: The tag firmware and version used during the deployment.

fields.name: weight
fields.description: Weight of the device with harness. Expressed in grams.

fields.name: attachment_type
fields.description: The way a tag is attached to an animal. Use values from [Movebank `attachment type` concept](https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000052) values if possible. Assumed to be `harness` if not provided. You can also provide the material used.

fields.name: readout_method
fields.description: The way the data are received from the tag. Use value from [Movebank `tag readout method` concept](https://vocab.nerc.ac.uk/collection/MVB/current/MVB000188/) if possible. Assumed to be `tag-retrieval` if not provided

fields.name: tag_comments
fields.description: Comments or notes about the tag, deployement or animal.

schema.name: twilights
schema.title: Twilights
schema.description: Table with twilights data infered from light data for all tags. Twilight data is typically computed with [`GeoPressureR::twilight_create()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/twilight_create.html).

fields.name: tag_id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the tag. Foreign key to [`tags.tag_id`](/GeoLocator-DP/core/tags#tag_id).

fields.name: twilight
fields.description: Date and time of the twilights. Formatted as an ISO 8601 string with timezone designator `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ`.

fields.name: rise
fields.description: Boolean value to indicate if the twilight is a sunrise (true) or sunset (false).

fields.name: label
fields.description: Labeling of the twilight data according to the [GeoPressureManual](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureManual/light-map.html#manual-labelling-of-twilight). The value `discard` indicates that the twilight should not be used for geopositioning. Any other value can be use without affecting the workflow, typically set to `stap_id` allows to visualize which twilight belong to the same stationary period on trainset.

schema.name: staps
schema.title: Stationary periods

fields.name: tag_id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the tag. Foreign key to [`tags.tag_id`](/GeoLocator-DP/core/tags#tag_id).

fields.name: stap_id
fields.description: Identifier of the stationary period to which the twilight belong to.

fields.name: start
fields.description: Date and time of the start of the stationary period. Formatted as an ISO 8601 string with timezone designator `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ`.

fields.name: end
fields.description: Date and time of the end of the stationary period. Formatted as an ISO 8601 string with timezone designator `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ`.

fields.name: known_lat
fields.description: Latitude of the known stationay period. This value is typically assigned by [`GeoPressureR::tag_set_map()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/tag_set_map).

fields.name: known_lon
fields.description: Longitude of the known stationay period. This value is typically assigned by [`GeoPressureR::tag_set_map()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/tag_set_map).

fields.name: include
fields.description: Boolean value to indicate if the stationary period should be included in the model. This value is typically assigned by [`GeoPressureR::tag_set_map()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/tag_set_map).

schema.name: paths
schema.title: Paths
schema.description:
A GeoPressureR `path` constains the positions of a bird's trajectory. 

{:.alert .alert-info}
In GeoPressureR, there are 4 types of path : `tag`, `most_likely`, `simulation` and `geopressureviz`. See [`paths.type`](#type) for more details. 

 `path` should always be used in combinaison with [`staps`](geopressurer/staps) which can be JOIN by `tag_id` and `stap_id`.

fields.name: tag_id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the tag. Foreign key to [`tags.tag_id`](/GeoLocator-DP/core/tags#tag_id).

fields.name: stap_id
fields.description: Identifier of the stationary period. Foreign key to [`staps.stap_id`](/GeoLocator-DP/geopressurer/staps#stap_id)

fields.name: type
fields.description:
One of:
 - `tag`: Create with [`tag2path()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/tag2path) from a likelihood map (e.g., ).
 - `most_likely`: Created with [`graph_most_likely()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/graph_most_likely.html)
- `simulation`: created by [`graph_simulation()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/graph_simulation): 
- `geopressureviz`: created by [`geopressureviz()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/geopressureviz).

fields.name: lat
fields.description: Latitude of the stationay period.

fields.name: lon
fields.description: Longitude of the stationay period.

fields.name: ind
fields.description: Indices of the 2D coordinate of the spatial map defined in [`GeoPressureR::tag_set_map()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/tag_set_map) (see also [`GeoPressureR::map_expand()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/map_expand). Useful to retrieve information on the graph (e.g. [`GeoPressureR::path2edge()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/path2edge). This value is the main way to create a path in GeoPressureR through [`GeoPressureR::ind2path()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/ind2path).

fields.name: j
fields.description: Only useful for `paths.type='simulation'`. Unique identifier for each trajectory, integer from `1` to `nj` as defined in [`GeoPressureR::graph_simulation()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/graph_simulation)`.

fields.name: interp
fields.description: Only useful for `paths.type='tag'`. Boolean value to indicate if the position was interpolated as defined by the `interp` parameter in [`GeoPressureR::tag2path()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/tag2path).

fields.name: known
fields.description: Boolean to indicate if the position is taken from a known value. Genearally, `TRUE` if [`staps.known_lat`](geopressurer/staps#known_lat) and [`staps.known_lon`](geopressurer/staps#known_lon) provided. Only different if `paths.type='tag'` and `use_known=FALSE` used in [`GeoPressureR::tag2path()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/tag2path).

schema.name: edges
schema.title: Edges
schema.description: In GeoPressureR, we uses `edges` (of a graph; See more infomration on the movement model in the [GeoPressureManual](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureManual/trajectory.html)) to extract flight information from GeoPressureP [`paths`](geopressurer/paths) using path2edge().

fields.name: tag_id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the tag. Foreign key to [`tags.tag_id`](/GeoLocator-DP/core/tags#tag_id).

fields.name: stap_s
fields.description: Identifier of the SOURCE stationary period. Foreign key to [`staps.stap_id`](/GeoLocator-DP/geopressurer/staps#stap_id)

fields.name: stap_t
fields.description: Identifier of the TARGET stationary period. Generally `stap_s+1` exept if `include=FALSE` for intermediate stap_id. Foreign key to [`staps.stap_id`](/GeoLocator-DP/geopressurer/staps#stap_id).

fields.name: lat_s
fields.description: Latitude of source the stationay period.

fields.name: lon_s
fields.description: Longitude of the source stationay period.

fields.name: lat_t
fields.description: Latitude of the target stationay period.

fields.name: lon_t
fields.description: Longitude of the target stationay period.

fields.name: s
fields.description: Index of the 2D coordinate of the source stap. See [`paths.ind`](geopressurer/paths#ind) for more information

fields.name: t
fields.description: Index of the 2D coordinate of the target stap. See [`paths.ind`](geopressurer/paths#ind) for more information

fields.name: j
fields.description: Unique identifier for each simulated trajectory, integer from `1` to `nj` as defined in [`GeoPressureR::graph_simulation()`](https://raphaelnussbaumer.com/GeoPressureR/reference/graph_simulation)`.

fields.name: start
fields.description: Date and time of the start of the flight. Formatted as an ISO 8601 string with timezone designator `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ`.

fields.name: end
fields.description: Date and time of the end of the flight. Formatted as an ISO 8601 string with timezone designator `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ`.

fields.name: n
fields.description: Number of flight.

fields.name: distance
fields.description: distance of the flight in km.

fields.name: bearing
fields.description: Direction of the flight in degree. 0 indicates north, positive indicate eastward direction, and negative indicate westward direction.

fields.name: gs_u
fields.description: The average groundspeed of the entire flight along the longitudinal axis (east-west direction), measured in kilometers per hour (km/h).

fields.name: gs_v
fields.description: The average groundspeed of the entire flight along the latitudinal axis (north-south direction), measured in kilometers per hour (km/h).

fields.name: ws_u
fields.description: The average windspeed of the entire flight along the longitudinal axis (east-west direction), measured in kilometers per hour (km/h).

fields.name: ws_v
fields.description: The average windspeed of the entire flight along the latitudinal axis (north-south direction), measured in kilometers per hour (km/h).

schema.name: observations
schema.title: observations
schema.description:
`observations.csv` is a [tabular data](https://datapackage.org/standard/glossary/#tabular-data) ressources from a GeoLocator Data Package containing any relevant [events](http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/Event) (datetime and location) that happened typically on the field and which providing important information for the analysis. These observations must typically include at least the equipment and, if appriate, the retrieval, but also any ringing control or direct sightings for instance.

{:.alert .alert-info}
You can think of `observations` as your log entries in your ringing book.

An observation is described by at least, the `datetime` of the observation, the location (`latitude` and `longitude`), the `ring_number` of the bird and the type of observations `observation_type` (e.g., equipement, retrieval or control). In addition, we require also `age` and `life_stage` (which can be set as unknown `U`).

 Also include the ringing events of any control group (e.g., birds ringed but not equipped with a tag).

fields.name: ring_number
fields.description: Ring number associated with the observed species. Foreign key to [`tags.ring_number`](/GeoLocator-DP/core/tags#ring_number).

fields.name: tag_id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the tag. `tag_id` is NOT required in this table so that you can enter any relevant ringed specied (e.g. control group). Foreign key to [`tags.tag_id`](/GeoLocator-DP/core/tags#tag_id).

fields.name: observation_type
fields.description:
Type of observation. 
 - `equipment`: Attachment of the tag on the bird
 - `retrieval`: Recapture and removal of the tag from the bird
 - `capture`: General case of capture of a bird while not deploying nor retriving a tag. This option covers the case of (1) recapturing a bird without a device (choose `missing` in `device_status`), (2) the case of a recapture happening after a `retrieval` or before and `equipment` and (3) and capture of individuals from the control group. Recapture/retrap are not hard coded in the dataset, but infered from consecutive capture of the same `ring_number`. 
 - `sighting`: Known presence of the bird on a date and location besides ringing (e.g. direct observation in the field with color ring)
 - `other`: Other type of observation. Avoid using this, and if using, always provide [`observation_comments`](core/observations/#observation_comments).

fields.name: datetime
fields.description: Date and time of the observation. Formatted as an ISO 8601 string (`YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ`).

fields.name: latitude
fields.description: Latitude of the observation location in decimal degrees, using the WGS84 datum.

fields.name: longitude
fields.description: Longitude of the observation location in decimal degrees, using the WGS84 datum.

fields.name: location_name
fields.description: Given name of the observation location. It might be helpful to keep a human-readable name of the location to differentiate the location of different observations more easily.

fields.name: device_status
fields.description: Status of the tag. Assumed to be `present` if not provided. Always provide [`observation_comments`](core/observations/#observation_comments) if using `unknown`.

fields.name: observer
fields.description: The name of the individual or group who made the observation.

fields.name: catching_method
fields.description:
Catching method according to [EURING Exchange Code](https://euring.org/files/documents/E2020ExchangeCodeV201.pdf#page=12). Generally, one of:
 - `M`: *M*isnet
 - `N`: on *n*est
 - `Z`: unknown

fields.name: life_stage
fields.description:
Life stage of the bird according to the age class in [EURING Exchange Code](https://euring.org/files/documents/E2020ExchangeCodeV201.pdf#page=14). 
- `0`: Unknown
- `1`: Pullus
- `2`: Full-grown
- `3`: 1yr
- `4`: >1yr
- `5`: 2yr
- `6`: >2yr

fields.name: sex
fields.description: Sex of the bird according to [EURING Exchange Code](https://euring.org/files/documents/E2020ExchangeCodeV201.pdf#page=13) as male `M`, female `F` or unknown `U`.

fields.name: condition
fields.description: Condition of bird follow a simplified version of [EURING Exchange Code](https://euring.org/files/documents/E2020ExchangeCodeV201.pdf#page=23). Assumed to be `alive` if not provided

fields.name: mass
fields.description: Mass of the tagged bird. See [EURING Exchange Code](https://euring.org/files/documents/E2020ExchangeCodeV201.pdf#page=27). Expressed in grams.

fields.name: wing_length
fields.description: Maximum chord measurement. See [EURING Exchange Code](https://euring.org/files/documents/E2020ExchangeCodeV201.pdf#page=26). Expressed in milimeters.

fields.name: additional_metric
fields.description: A list of additional measurements, facts, characteristics, or assertions about the record. Recommended best practice is to use a key:value encoding schema for a data interchange format such as JSON.

fields.name: observation_comments
fields.description: Comments or notes about the observation.
